steps.title: Calculate the area of rectangle X and use it to find triangle Y's area
steps.content: Rectangle area: $$24 \times 17.5 = 420 \text{ mm}^2$$ Since the rectangle's area is 3 times the triangle's area: $$\text{Triangle area} = \frac{420}{3} = 140 \text{ mm}^2$$

steps.title: Apply the triangle area formula to find height
steps.content: For a right triangle, Area $= \frac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times \text{height}$. Substituting: $$140 = \frac{1}{2} \times 20 \times h$$ $$140 = 10h$$ $$h = 14$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
